user_message: Just came out of territory planning with Blue Yonder Airlines. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:06:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:17:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-N trap (No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-N failure mode (No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-N -- No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
